articles.description: It's the latest salvo in an escalating battle between state regulators and an industry that claims it's not beholden to them.

articles.title: Mistral bets on ‘build-your-own AI’ as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
articles.description: Mistral Forge lets enterprises train custom AI models from scratch on their own data, challenging rivals that rely on fine-tuning and retrieval-based approaches.

articles.title: Why Garry Tan’s Claude Code setup has gotten so much love, and hate
articles.description: Thousands of people are trying Garry Tan's Claude Code setup, which was shared on GitHub. And everyone has an opinion: even Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini.

articles.title: Apple rolls out first ‘background security’ update for iPhones, iPads, and Macs to fix Safari bug
articles.description: Apple's first-ever "background security improvement" fixes a vulnerability in its Safari browser running its latest software.

articles.title: Kagi brings its ‘small web’ of a human-only internet to mobile devices
articles.description: Kagi's "Small Web" offers a handpicked collection of more than 30,000 non-commercial, human-authored websites, including personal blogs, webcomics, and independent videos.

articles.title: The Pentagon is developing alternatives to Anthropic, report says
articles.description: After their dramatic falling-out, it doesn't seem as though Anthropic and the Pentagon are getting back together.

articles.title: BuzzFeed debuts AI slop apps in bid for new revenue
articles.description: BuzzFeed unveiled new AI-powered social apps at SXSW, but its demos drew muted reactions.

articles.title: Google’s data center power playbook comes into focus
articles.description: Google has long procured clean power for its operations and data centers, but recent deals show the company is changing tactics.

articles.title: Meet Vurt, the mobile-first streaming platform for indie filmmakers embracing vertical video
articles.description: Micro-drama apps make billions in consumer spending, so VURT launched its streaming app to allow indie filmmakers to capitalize on the vertical video trend.

articles.title: Google’s Personal Intelligence feature is expanding to all US users
articles.description: Personal Intelligence allows Google's AI assistant to tap into your Google ecosystem, such as Gmail and Google Photos, to provide more tailored responses.

articles.title: Stryker says it’s restoring systems after pro-Iran hackers wiped thousands of employee devices
articles.description: The hack, which brought ongoing widespread disruption to the company's operations, is thought to be the first major cyberattack in the United States in response to the Trump administration's war in Iran.

articles.title: OpenAI expands government footprint with AWS deal, report says
articles.description: OpenAI has reportedly signed a partnership with AWS to sell its AI systems to the U.S. government for classified and unclassified work, marking an expansion beyond its Pentagon deal last month.

articles.title: AI’s ‘boys’ club’ could widen the wealth gap for women, says Rana el Kaliouby
articles.description: AI investor Rana el Kaliouby warns that if women are shut out of AI funding and leadership, the consequences will be grim.

articles.title: Oura enters India’s smart ring market with the Ring 4
articles.description: Finnish smart ring maker Oura is finally launching in India, taking on local rivals such as Ultrahuman in a relatively young smart ring market that is becoming price-sensitive thanks to an influx of low-cost options.

articles.title: World launches tool to verify humans behind AI shopping agents
articles.description: As AI agents take the reins for online shoppers, Sam Altman's unconventional startup is looking to expand its verification offerings to support agentic commerce.

articles.title: MacBook Neo, AirPods Max 2, iPhone 17e, and everything else Apple announced this month
articles.description: From a new iPhone to a budget-friendly MacBook, Apple announced a series of new products this week.

articles.title: Niv-AI exits stealth to wring more power performance out of GPUs
articles.description: The company raised $12 million in seed funding to measure and manage GPU power surges.

articles.title: H&M wants to make clothing from CO2 using this startup’s tech
articles.description: Rubi has developed an enzymatic process to turn carbon dioxide into cellulose that's ready to be spun into lyocell or viscose.

articles.title: Gamma adds AI image-generation tools in bid to take on Canva and Adobe
articles.description: The company's new product, called Gamma Imagine, will let users employ text prompts to create brand-specific assets like interactive charts and visualizations, marketing collateral, social graphics, and infographics.

articles.title: Amazon adds 1-hour and 3-hour delivery options in the US
articles.description: The e-commerce giant is making more than 90,000 items available via this new delivery system. If an item can be delivered to a user within one or three hours, they'll see a label saying so next to that item on the Amazon app.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Python 3.15's JIT is now back on track
URL: https://fidget-spinner.github.io/posts/jit-on-track.html
Python's performance story just got credible again
After months of uncertainty, the JIT compiler for Python 3.15 is proceeding. This matters because Python's performance ceiling has been the single biggest architectural constraint pushing workloads to Rust/Go rewrites. If the JIT delivers even 2x improvements on compute-bound code, it changes the build-vs-buy calculus for every data platform.

### Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking
URL: https://github.com/adammiribyan/zeroboot
Cold start latency just became a solvable problem
Copy-on-write memory forking enabling sub-millisecond VM spin-up is precisely the infrastructure primitive that serverless and AI inference platforms need. Current cold starts of 100-500ms are the hidden tax killing edge deployment economics. Watch for acquisition interest from Cloudflare, Fly.io, or the hyperscalers.

### Microsoft's 'unhackable' Xbox One has been hacked by 'Bliss'
URL: https://www.tomshardware.com/video-games/console-gaming/microsofts-unhackable-xbox-one-has-been-hacked-by-bliss-the-2013-console-finally-fell-to-voltage-glitching-allowing-the-loading-of-unsigned-code-at-every-level
Thirteen years of security assumptions just collapsed
Voltage glitching attacks bypassing hardware security modules have implications far beyond gaming. The Xbox One's security architecture was considered gold-standard for embedded systems. Every IoT device, automotive ECU, and secure enclave using similar designs should now be assumed vulnerable to determined attackers with physical access.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### Mistral bets on 'build-your-own AI' as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/17/mistral-forge-nvidia-gtc-build-your-own-ai-enterprise/
Mistral just pivoted from model provider to platform company
Forge represents Mistral's recognition that competing on foundation model benchmarks is a losing game against OpenAI's capital base. Training custom models from scratch on enterprise data is the differentiated wedge—it addresses data sovereignty concerns and creates genuine switching costs. The real question: can European regulatory capture translate into enterprise revenue before the cash runs out?

### Kalshi's legal troubles pile up, as Arizona files first ever criminal charges over 'illegal gambling business'
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/17/kalshis-legal-troubles-pile-up-as-arizona-files-first-ever-criminal-charges-over-illegal-gambling-business/
Prediction markets' regulatory arbitrage thesis is being stress-tested
Criminal charges in Arizona represent a significant escalation from civil enforcement. Kalshi's entire business model depends on maintaining the fiction that event contracts are financial instruments, not gambling. If state AGs successfully prosecute under gambling statutes, the $100M+ raised becomes a case study in regulatory capture failure.

### OpenAI expands government footprint with AWS deal, report says
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/17/openai-expands-government-footprint-with-aws-deal/
OpenAI is building the Palantir playbook for AI
Selling to government through AWS infrastructure solves OpenAI's FedRAMP problem while giving Amazon a differentiated AI offering. This is a defensive move against Anthropic's government relationships post-Pentagon fallout. The classified workload angle suggests OpenAI is positioning for defense and intelligence contracts that could dwarf commercial revenue.

### The Pentagon is developing alternatives to Anthropic, report says
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/17/the-pentagon-is-developing-alternatives-to-anthropic-report-says/
DoD just signaled they'll build rather than buy AI
The Pentagon developing in-house alternatives after the Anthropic falling-out is the clearest indicator yet that AI safety positioning is incompatible with defense contracts. This creates a bifurcated market: safety-focused labs serving commercial enterprise, and government-aligned labs (or internal teams) serving national security. Watch for defense contractor acquisitions of smaller AI labs.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Heaps do lie: Debugging a memory leak in vLLM
URL: https://mistral.ai/news/debugging-memory-leak-in-vllm
Infrastructure engineering for inference is becoming a core competency
Mistral publishing a detailed post-mortem on vLLM memory leaks reveals the brutal operational reality of running inference at scale. The specific debugging methodology—heap profilers misleading, requiring kernel-level tracing—demonstrates that AI deployment is now a systems engineering problem, not a model engineering problem. This is the talent gap PE should be underwriting.

### A tale about fixing eBPF spinlock issues in the Linux kernel
URL: https://www.rovarma.com/articles/a-tale-about-fixing-ebpf-spinlock-issues-in-the-linux-kernel/
The eBPF ecosystem is maturing into production-grade infrastructure
eBPF spinlock fixes matter because eBPF has become the observability and security layer for cloud-native infrastructure. Companies like Cilium, Isovalent (now Cisco), and the entire service mesh ecosystem depend on eBPF reliability. Kernel-level bug fixes in this subsystem directly impact the stability of every major cloud deployment.

### Why AI systems don't learn – On autonomous learning from cognitive science
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15381
Academic consensus is forming around current AI limitations
A cognitive science perspective on why AI systems don't truly learn autonomously is useful framing for the hype cycle. If the paper's thesis holds—that current architectures are fundamentally incapable of genuine autonomous learning—it suggests the next wave of AI progress requires architectural breakthroughs, not just scale. Investment implications: infrastructure plays remain safe, but pure model companies face technical risk.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The week's most important pattern is the fracturing of AI's unified market thesis. Government and commercial AI are diverging into separate regulatory and ethical regimes, with OpenAI chasing defense dollars while Anthropic gets cut out. Meanwhile, infrastructure constraints—GPU power management, memory leaks in inference stacks, cold start latency—are becoming the binding constraints on AI deployment, suggesting the real alpha is in systems engineering rather than model capabilities. The Python JIT and sub-millisecond VM work hint at a broader theme: the next performance frontier is in runtime efficiency, not raw compute.
